findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find regex compile operations with static patterns
 * @description Finds all re.compile operations with static regex patterns that might be used for validation
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity recommendation
 * @id py/find-regex-compile
 * @tags regex
 *       validation
 */

import python
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

from DataFlow::CallCfgNode call, DataFlow::Node pattern
where
  call = API::moduleImport("re").getMember("compile").getACall() and
  pattern = call.getArg(0)
select call, "Regex compile operation with pattern at: " + pattern.getLocation().toString()
findings.hypothesis: Find all regex compile operations to identify potential ReDoS vulnerabilities in validation patterns

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find email validation functions
 * @description Finds functions that validate email addresses, particularly those using regex
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity recommendation
 * @id py/find-email-validation
 * @tags email
 *       validation
 */

import python
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

from Function f
where
  (
    f.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%email%") or
    f.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%validate%")
  )
  and exists(Call c |
    c.getScope() = f and
    (
      c.getFunc().(Attribute).getObject().(Name).getId() = "re" or
      c.getFunc().(Name).getId().matches("%compile%") or
      c.getFunc().(Attribute).getName().matches("%match%") or
      c.getFunc().(Attribute).getName().matches("%search%") or
      c.getFunc().(Attribute).getName().matches("%fullmatch%")
    )
  )
select f, "Email validation function '" + f.getName() + "' at " + f.getLocation().getFile().getRelativePath() + ":" + f.getLocation().getStartLine().toString()
findings.hypothesis: Find email validation functions that use regex operations

message.text: Email validation function '_build_pretty_email_regex' at pydantic/networks.py:389
Email validation function 'validate_email' at pydantic/networks.py:400
Email validation function 'validate' at pydantic/v1/networks.py:267
Email validation function 'validate_host' at pydantic/v1/networks.py:342
Email validation function 'validate_email' at pydantic/v1/networks.py:707
Email validation function 'validate' at pydantic/v1/types.py:434
Email validation function 'validate' at pydantic/v1/types.py:1096
Email validation function '_validate' at pydantic/types.py:960

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Find regex patterns in networks module
 * @description Find all regex compile calls in networks.py files
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity recommendation
 * @id py/find-network-regex-patterns
 * @tags security
 */

import python

from Call call, StrConst pattern
where
  call.getFunc().(Attribute).getObject().(Name).getId() = "re" and
  call.getFunc().(Attribute).getName() = "compile" and
  call.getLocation().getFile().getBaseName() = "networks.py" and
  pattern = call.getArg(0)
select call, "Regex pattern: " + pattern.getText()
findings.hypothesis: Find regex patterns used in networks.py to identify potentially vulnerable patterns

message.text: Regex pattern: (\[\w \]*?) *<(.*)> *

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Email validation regex without length check
 * @description Email validation using regex without prior length validation can lead to ReDoS
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id py/email-regex-no-length-check
 * @tags security
 *       external/cwe/cwe-1333
 */

import python
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

from DataFlow::CallCfgNode regexCall, Function emailFunc
where
  // Find regex operations (match, search, fullmatch, compile)
  (
    regexCall = API::moduleImport("re").getMember(["match", "search", "fullmatch", "compile"]).getACall()
  ) and
  // The regex call is inside a function with "email" or "validate" in its name
  regexCall.getScope() = emailFunc and
  (
    emailFunc.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%email%") or
    emailFunc.getName().toLowerCase().matches("%validate%")
  ) and
  // Check if there's no length check before the regex operation
  not exists(DataFlow::CallCfgNode lenCall |
    lenCall = API::builtin("len").getACall() and
    lenCall.getScope() = emailFunc
  )
select regexCall, "Regex operation in email validation function '" + emailFunc.getName() + "' without length validation"
findings.hypothesis: Email validation functions using regex without length checks are vulnerable to ReDoS attacks

message.text: Regex operation in email validation function '_build_pretty_email_regex' without length validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Email validation regex without length check
 * @description Email validation using regex on unbounded input can lead to ReDoS
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id py/email-validation-redos
 * @tags security
 *        external/cwe/cwe-1333
 */

import python
import semmle.python.ApiGraphs

from DataFlow::CallCfgNode call, Function func
where
  // Find calls to validate_email function
  func.getName() = "validate_email" and
  call.getFunction().getScope() = func and
  // Check if it's a regex operation
  (
    call = API::moduleImport("re").getMember(["match", "search", "fullmatch"]).getACall() or
    exists(DataFlow::AttrRead attr |
      attr.getAttributeName() in ["match", "search", "fullmatch"] and
      call.getFunction() = attr
    )
  )
select call, "Regex operation in email validation function '" + func.getName() + "' may be vulnerable to ReDoS without length validation"
findings.hypothesis: Email validation functions using regex operations without length checks may be vulnerable to ReDoS attacks

message.text: Regex operation in email validation function 'validate_email' may be vulnerable to ReDoS without length validation
Regex operation in email validation function 'validate_email' may be vulnerable to ReDoS without length validation

findings.codeql_raw_query:
/**
 * @name Email validation without length check before regex
 * @description Email validation that applies regex without checking input length can lead to ReDoS
 * @kind problem
 * @problem.severity warning
 * @id py/email-validation-missing-length-check
 * @tags security
 *        external/cwe/cwe-1333
 */

import python

from Function func, Call regexCall
where
  func.getName() = "validate_email" and
  regexCall.getScope() = func and
  (
    regexCall.getFunc().(Attribute).getObject().(Name).getId() = "re" and
    regexCall.getFunc().(Attribute).getName() in ["match", "search", "fullmatch"]
    or
    exists(Attribute attr |
      attr = regexCall.getFunc() and
      attr.getName() in ["match", "search", "fullmatch"]
    )
  ) and
  // Check if there's no length validation before the regex call
  not exists(Compare cmp |
    cmp.getScope() = func and
    cmp.getLocation().getStartLine() < regexCall.getLocation().getStartLine() and
    exists(Call lenCall |
      lenCall = cmp.getASubExpression*() and
      lenCall.getFunc().(Name).getId() = "len"
    )
  )
select regexCall, "Email validation applies regex without checking input length, potentially vulnerable to ReDoS"
findings.hypothesis: Email validation functions apply regex patterns without validating input length first, making them vulnerable to ReDoS attacks

message.text: Email validation applies regex without checking input length, potentially vulnerable to ReDoS
Email validation applies regex without checking input length, potentially vulnerable to ReDoS
